title: Manual password reset by admin
description: Allow an admin to set a new password for another user directly from the user management screen, with no email flow (per MVP scope — SMTP-based reset is backlog V2).
acceptanceCriteria: `users.resetPassword` (adminProcedure mutation) accepts `{ userId, newPassword }`, hashes it via `hashPassword`, and updates `users.senha_hash`
After reset, all existing sessions for that user are invalidated (deleted from `sessions`), forcing re-login with the new password
User management screen (TASK-13) has a 'Reset password' action per user opening a small form for the new password
The new password is never displayed back to the admin after submission — only a success confirmation

steps.details: Add to `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/users.ts`: `resetPassword: adminProcedure.input(z.object({ userId: z.string(), newPassword: z.string().min(8) })).mutation(...)` that hashes and updates `senha_hash`.
Within the same mutation, delete all `sessions` rows where `user_id` matches the target user, so old sessions can't continue using the previous credential context.

steps.description: Add reset UI to user management screen
steps.details: In `apps/admin/src/features/users/UsersList.tsx` (from TASK-13), add a per-row 'Redefinir senha' button opening a small modal/inline form with a single password field, calling `users.resetPassword`.

steps.description: Show success confirmation without echoing the password
steps.details: On success, close the form and show a toast/banner 'Senha redefinida com sucesso' — never render the submitted password back in the UI.
Using Playwright: as admin, reset an editor's password; log in as that editor with the old password and confirm failure; log in with the new password and confirm success.

technicalNotes: This is intentionally the only password-recovery mechanism in the MVP — 'esqueci minha senha' via SMTP is explicitly backlog V2 per the PRD.
